title: Increased chance of Prion Disease (PrP 129 Met homozygote)
summary: Increased chance of Prion Disease (PrP 129 Met homozygote)
detail: This genotype encodes for a homozygous Methionine at position 129 of PRNP, the Prion Protein gene (PrP-129MM). Unlike the protective M/V heterozgotes and V homozygous, homozygosity at this residue is associated with an increased susceptibility to sporadic CJD and and confers susceptibility against vCJD. All reported cases of vCJD, (linked to ingestion of 'mad cow disease' contaminated meat) have been 129 Met homozygous
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
conditions: Increased chance of Prion Disease (PrP 129 Met homozygote)

This genotype encodes for a homozygous Methionine at position 129 of PRNP, the Prion Protein gene (PrP-129MM). Unlike the protective M/V heterozgotes and V homozygous, homozygosity at this residue is associated with an increased susceptibility to sporadic CJD and and confers susceptibility against vCJD. All reported cases of vCJD, (linked to ingestion of 'mad cow disease' contaminated meat) have been 129 Met homozygous
release: SNPedia cached page export; page timestamp 2016-08-29T12:15:40Z
notes: SNPedia is supplementary and should not be treated as a primary clinical source.

title: Resistance to vCJD (PrP 129 Met/Val heterozygote), 4.6x reduced risk of sporadic CJD and 0.87...
summary: Resistance to vCJD (PrP 129 Met/Val heterozygote), 4.6x reduced risk of sporadic CJD and 0.87x reduced risk for late-onset Alzheimer in a Caucasian population
detail: This genotype codes for Met/Val heterozygote at codon 129 in PrP, the Prion Protein. This is strongly protective against development of sporadic, and confers resistance against vCJD. Studies in Papua New Guinea showed that this genotype prevents transmission of kuru , a form of Prion disease transmitted by cannibalism. So eat as many brains as you want!
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
conditions: Resistance to vCJD (PrP 129 Met/Val heterozygote), 4.6x reduced risk of sporadic CJD and 0.87x reduced risk for late-onset Alzheimer in a Caucasian population
